publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 153-157. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Friday Afternoon

My Dearest Mate. I am so happy altogether, & so well & nice today, that I don't know how to put my chirps & gaiety on paper so as to convey the thrush's glee to her dear Mate. I have quantities to tell him.-- I have been

making acquaintance with Sir William Molesworth. I rode with him two days ago, which was my first introduction to him,-- arranged by Mr Duppa. We took to each other much, & I was most agreeably surprised & disappointed in his gentlemanlike respectful manners, & the absence of everything like self-sufficiency or

conceit. He has just been with me today, & we have had a long & highly interesting conversation on science, mathematics, metaphysics &c. I have invited him to Ockham (of which I hope you will not disapprove). He has a most extraordinary intellect, & I consider him a valuable addition to my flock.--
Yesterday we all

went to Norwood as proposed. There was nothing in H's manners to disapprove of or regret. At Norwood we met Sir T & Lady Acland, old Mr Fortescue, Henry Fortescue, in short all the collateral Fortescues almost.--
In returning home, we paid the intended visit to the Astrologer at Dulwich; & were
altogether highly entertained there. The particulars of the visit, I cannot communicate now in a letter.--
This evening I go to the German Opera with Dr Kay.--
I have been very much with the Hen since you went. She seems to like & approve me, & all my plans & intentions,

as far as I have explained them; and especially my intended & increasing intercourse & projects with Dr Kay. She has been further discussing me with him, & she tells me she is astonished at his knowledge, penetration, & judgment respecting me; & much gratified to see the honest & disinterested
friendship he entertains for the Avis.--
Pray love the happy Bird, who never can be grateful enough for the relations & friends she is so singularly blessed with; from ou downwards.--
I was disappointed to have no letter from you this morning, but on calculating, I rather believe I could not
easily have heard before tomorrow. I write, as you may perceive, in haste; & am scrawling in a very Bird-claw [claw underlined twice] -like way.--
We have had much rain; but it is fine this afternoon. I am going to ride out with Mr Duppa.--
Dearest Mate, Good Bye. The Bird chirps at ou.
I had a long visit from Greig again yesterday morning
in which he repeated his urgency about the Chair-man-ship, & his conviction of a unanimous election for you.--
Avis Phoenix
P.S. I hope you have not thought me negligent for not writing yesterday I was much hurried.--
